pattern: A call stack of running function ids, plus a remembered last timestamp, lets each log event credit the elapsed slice to whichever function currently sits on top.
hints: Parse each log into id, kind, and timestamp; keep a stack of currently running function ids.
When a function starts, the time since the previous event belongs to whoever was on top before; then push the new id.
On a start at time t, add t - prev to the current top (if any), push id, set prev = t. On an end at time t, add t - prev + 1 to the popped id, then set prev = t + 1.

edge_inputs.description: second statement example, recursion on one function
third statement example, recursion then a call
function with zero gap end at same start
function never runs gets zero time
two sibling calls back to back

stress.description: random well-formed nested call logs over several functions
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 10)
    depth_target = rng.randint(1, n)
    pairs = max(1, size // 2)
    logs = []
    stack = []
    t = 0
    for _ in range(pairs):
        # decide to start or end
        if stack and (len(stack) >= depth_target or rng.random() < 0.5):
            fid = stack.pop()
            logs.append('%d:end:%d' % (fid, t))
        else:
            fid = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
            stack.append(fid)
            logs.append('%d:start:%d' % (fid, t))
        t += rng.randint(1, 5)
    while stack:
        fid = stack.pop()
        logs.append('%d:end:%d' % (fid, t))
        t += rng.randint(1, 5)
    if len(logs) < 2:
        logs = ['0:start:0', '0:end:1']
        n = max(n, 1)
    return [n, logs]
stress.note: stack discipline guarantees every start has a matching end
